name.description: Unique provider name used to reference this provider.

provider_type.description: Optional provider type (e.g. "openai", "anthropic"); inferred from name if absent.

api_key.description: API key used to authenticate with the upstream provider.

base_url.description: Override the upstream base URL (defaults to the provider's well-known URL).

models.description: Models served by this provider; empty defers to the provider catalog.

default_model.description: Default model used when the request omits an explicit model.

model_map.description: Per-provider mapping from logical model name to upstream model name.

weight.description: Weight used by weighted routing strategies.

priority.description: Priority used by priority-based routing (lower runs first).

enabled.description: Whether this provider is eligible for routing.

max_retries.description: Maximum retry attempts on transient upstream failures.

organization.description: Organization identifier (used by providers that scope keys per org).

api_version.description: API version header value (used by Anthropic and Azure OpenAI).

host_override.description: Override the `Host` header sent to the AI provider. Defaults to the provider's base URL hostname (api.openai.com, api.anthropic.com, ...). Set this when fronting the provider through a custom domain.

disable_forwarded_host_header.description: When true, suppress the `X-Forwarded-Host` header that the proxy would otherwise set to the client's original `Host` whenever it rewrites the upstream `Host`.

allow_private_base_url.description: Allow this provider's `base_url` to point at a private/loopback address. Defaults to `false`: a `base_url` resolving to a loopback, link-local, or RFC1918 target is rejected at config load as an SSRF risk. Set `true` for a local model server (Ollama, vLLM, LM Studio on `127.0.0.1`/LAN). Non-`http(s)` schemes (`file://`, ...) are always rejected regardless.

no_prompt_training.description: Operator's declaration that this provider does not retain or train on the prompts sent to it, per the provider's published data-handling agreement. There is no standardized per-request training opt-out header across providers, so SBproxy treats this as a deployment-level fact: a request that opts out of training (the `x-sbproxy-disallow-prompt-training` signal) is routed only to providers marked here. Defaults to `false`.

serve.description: WOR-1652: optional local model-serving block. When set, the gateway itself hosts the models (pull weights, fit an engine to the GPU, supervise it) and registers them as local providers ahead of any cloud fallback, instead of proxying `base_url` to an already-running server. `None` keeps the proxy-only behavior. The lifecycle half (spawn/evict) ships in phases of the model-host epic; this field is the config surface it reads.

ModelHostConfig.description: The `serve:` block: the local models plus host-wide policy.

models.description: Models to serve locally. Registered as local providers ahead of any cloud fallback.

catalog_file.description: Support: preview. Optional path to an operator catalog file, replacing the built-in certified catalog for id resolution.

cache_dir.description: Directory for the content-addressed weight cache. `None` uses the platform default (`$HF_HOME` / `~/.cache/sbproxy/models`).

cache_budget_gib.description: Support: config_only. Disk budget in GiB for the weight cache before GC. `None` means unbounded (operator manages the disk).

eviction.description: What to do under VRAM pressure. Defaults to LRU eviction.

engines.description: Support: preview. Per-engine provisioning (how to acquire each engine binary). Absent engines use the default (resolve from `PATH`).

max_concurrent_requests.description: Cap on concurrently dispatched served-lane requests (WOR-1679). When set, the gateway holds an admission gate in front of the local engine: requests beyond the cap wait in a priority queue (a virtual key's `priority:` lane orders the queue, interactive first), and an interactive request with a cloud fallback in the same provider array spills to it instead of queuing. `None` (the default) disables the gate; the engine's internal queue is the only limit, exactly as before.

queue_timeout_ms.description: Support: preview. How long a queued request waits for a served-lane slot before the attempt fails over to the next provider (or errors when no fallback exists). Milliseconds; defaults to 30000. Read only when `max_concurrent_requests` is set.

ServeEntry.description: One model an operator wants the gateway to serve locally.

model.description: Catalog id (`qwen3-32b`) or an explicit `hf:Org/Repo:QUANT` reference. Resolved by [`crate::catalog`].

name.description: The model id every other plane sees (WOR-1683): routing, `allowed_models`, rate limits, budgets, aliases. Defaults to the catalog id in `model`; **required** when `model` is a raw `hf:` reference (there is no catalog id to borrow). See [`Self::effective_name`].

engine.description: Support: preview. Engine to serve it with. Defaults to `auto` (resolved per model at boot from the weight format and what is installed).

keep_alive.description: Support: preview. Idle time before the engine is unloaded to free VRAM. Go duration syntax (`10m`, `1h`); `None` means never auto-unload.

max_context.description: Support: preview. Context length to plan VRAM for and pass to the engine. `None` uses the model's declared max (clamped by the fit planner to what actually fits).

extra_args.description: Support: preview. Extra engine flags appended to the templated args. Values only, no shell: each entry is passed as one argv element.

kv_quant.description: Support: preview. KV-cache quantization. Defaults to `Auto` (the weight quant's default KV dtype).

speculative.description: Support: preview. Speculative decoding. `None` disables it.

chunked_prefill.description: Support: preview. Chunked-prefill settings. `None` uses the engine default.

lora_adapters.description: Support: preview. LoRA adapters served over this base model.

pinned.description: Support: preview. Keep this model resident: it is never evicted to make room for another (WOR-1672). Use for a latency-critical model that must stay hot. A set of pinned models is therefore never split. Admitting a new model that only fits by evicting a pinned one is rejected instead.

tool_call_parser.description: Support: preview. vLLM tool-call parser to enable auto tool-choice (WOR-1668), e.g. `hermes` (Qwen), `llama3_json`, `mistral`. When set, the engine launches with `--enable-auto-tool-choice --tool-call-parser <name>` so `tool_choice: auto` requests work; without it vLLM rejects auto tool-choice. `None` leaves tool calling off.

swap_space_gib.description: Support: preview. CPU KV-cache tier size in GiB (WOR-1687): vLLM's `--swap-space`, the CPU pool it spills GPU KV blocks to under pressure so a longer effective context / larger batch survives beyond GPU VRAM. `None` uses the engine default.

cpu_offload_gib.description: Support: preview. GiB of model weights to keep in CPU RAM (WOR-1687): vLLM's `--cpu-offload-gb`, trading PCIe bandwidth for VRAM so a model that does not fit can still load. `None` disables offload.

max_loras.description: Support: preview. Max LoRA adapters resident on the engine at once (WOR-1673). When set below the number of configured `lora_adapters`, the engine loads adapters on demand and evicts the least-recently used past this cap (dynamic paging), rather than preloading all of them. `None` preloads every configured adapter (static), which suits a small, fixed adapter set.

gguf_file.description: Support: preview. For a llama.cpp GGUF model, the exact GGUF filename to serve from a multi-file repo (WOR-1656), e.g. `Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct-Q4_K_M.gguf`. With the `weights` feature sbproxy pre-fetches this file and passes llama.cpp a local `--model`, so it needs no curl-enabled build and never guesses the quant. Without pre-fetch it becomes `--hf-file` so llama.cpp downloads the right file. `None` lets llama.cpp resolve the repo default (fine only for a single-file repo).

EngineChoice.description: The engine an operator asks for on a model (WOR-1684). Unlike [`EngineKind`] (the resolved identity), this includes `Auto`, which the runtime resolves per model at boot from the weight format and what is installed. `Auto` is the default so a minimal `serve:` block does not have to name an engine.

oneOf.description: Resolve the engine from the weights and the environment.
Force vLLM.
Force llama.cpp.

Force the in-process embedded engine (WOR-1658). `Auto` never resolves to this; the embedded engine is an explicit opt-in because it changes the deployment model (no subprocess) and is only present when the `embedded` feature is compiled.

KvCacheQuant.description: KV-cache quantization for a served model (WOR-1676). Quantizing the KV cache multiplies effective KV capacity, so a model can fit a longer context or a larger batch on the same VRAM at some quality cost. `Auto` keeps the cache at the weight quant's default (f16 for most, fp8 when serving FP8).

oneOf.description: Follow the weight quant's default KV dtype.
16-bit KV (the safe, full-quality default).
8-bit float KV. Needs FP8-capable kernels.
8-bit integer KV.
4-bit KV (maximum capacity, largest quality hit).

SpeculativeConfig.description: Speculative-decoding settings for a served model.

num_speculative_tokens.description: How many tokens to propose per step.

SpecMethod.description: How a model does speculative decoding (WOR-1674). Speculation wins when the batch is memory-bound (low load) and loses when it is compute-bound (full batch), so the runtime load-gates it; this is the config that says which method to use when it is on.

oneOf.description: A separate small draft model proposes tokens.
N-gram / prompt-lookup speculation (no draft model).

ChunkedPrefill.description: Chunked-prefill settings (WOR-1678). Chunked prefill trades a larger prefill chunk (higher throughput) against time-to-first-token (lower with smaller chunks). Set `max_batched_tokens` directly, or set `target_ttft_ms` to have the planner pick a chunk size.

max_batched_tokens.description: Explicit prefill chunk size (engine `max-num-batched-tokens`). `None` leaves it to the auto-tune or the engine default.

target_ttft_ms.description: A TTFT SLO in milliseconds; when set (and no explicit chunk size), the planner chooses a chunk size to hold it.

LoraAdapter.description: A LoRA adapter served over a base model (WOR-1673). Clients request it by `name`; the runtime hot-loads it over the resident base with an LRU adapter cache rather than swapping the base model.

name.description: The model name clients request to hit this adapter.

source.description: Adapter source: an `hf:Org/Repo` reference or a local path.

EvictionPolicy.description: What to do when VRAM is needed and an idle model is resident.

oneOf.description: Evict the least-recently-used idle model to make room (default).
Never evict; reject a new model when VRAM is full. Predictable residency for a pinned single model.

EngineProvisioning.description: Provisioning for one engine (how to acquire it), keyed by engine in [`ModelHostConfig::engines`].

image.description: Pinned container image (a tag or digest, never `:latest`) for [`EngineLaunchMethod::Container`]. Rejected at plan time if it ends in `:latest` or has no tag.

acquire.description: How to acquire the binary when [`EngineLaunchMethod::Binary`] (WOR-1801). `None` keeps the pre-WOR-1801 behaviour: resolve from `PATH` only.

EngineLaunchMethod.description: How an engine binary is acquired (WOR-1684). Engine *identity* stays the allowlisted [`EngineKind`]; only the acquisition method is configurable, so the config-spawn security posture (no arbitrary `cmd:`) is unchanged.

oneOf.description: Resolve the engine binary from `PATH`.
Run the engine from a pinned container image.
Run the engine from a managed uv/venv (vLLM's Python path).

EngineAcquire.description: The `acquire:` block on a binary engine's provisioning (WOR-1801): how to obtain the binary. Engine *identity* stays the allowlisted [`EngineKind`]; only the acquisition method is configurable, so the config-spawn posture (no arbitrary `cmd:`) is unchanged.

source.description: Where the binary comes from. Defaults to a pinned release.

version.description: Release tag to pin (for `release`); `None` uses the built-in default pin. Never `latest`.

sha256.description: Expected sha256 hex of the release asset. Verifies the download when set; a `release` fetch without it logs a warning.

AcquireSource.description: How a *binary* engine (llama.cpp today) is obtained when it is not already on `PATH` (WOR-1801). Container / venv acquisition is a separate [`EngineLaunchMethod`]; this covers the single-binary path.

oneOf.description: A pinned prebuilt release for the platform + accel, sha256 verified when a digest is pinned (default). PATH is always tried first.
An operator-installed binary at an explicit `path`.

Provision via `uvx` (`uv tool run`): fetch the `uv` single binary and run the engine in a cached, ephemeral environment that uv sets up on first use, bringing its own Python if needed. This is the acquisition path for vLLM, which is a Python package rather than a single-binary release. `version` pins the *engine package* version (the vLLM version); the `uv` version is pinned internally.

EngineAccel.description: Hardware-acceleration flavour to acquire a binary engine build for (WOR-1801). `Auto` picks from the host GPU: Apple gets the Metal build (built into the macOS asset), an NVIDIA Linux box gets the Vulkan build (ggml-org ships no CUDA Linux prebuilt), everything else gets the CPU build.

oneOf.description: Detect from the host GPU.
CUDA. For llama.cpp on Linux this resolves to the Vulkan prebuilt (no upstream CUDA Linux asset); a CUDA container or source build is the alternative.
Vulkan (a portable GPU path on Linux).
Apple Metal (built into the macOS asset).
CPU-only.
